method: Document content-line position test for anonymous token type U+1076B (t=='other', '\U0001076b' in raw). Content-lines = maximal runs of content tokens (word/num/other) bounded by nl/START/END (div does not flush). Testable inscriptions: L>=3 content-lines AND >=1 marker-bearing line. Primary metric = PERIPHERAL rate (li==0 OR li==L-1). Null: per-inscription uniform re-placement of each marker's li in {0..L-1}, independent, preserving marker count and L; >=500 reshuffles; one-sided perm p (enrichment). Heading (li==0) and colophon (li==L-1) tested separately vs uniform 1/L.
result: U+1076B is strongly enriched at the document periphery (first OR last content-line) of multi-line Linear A inscriptions, cross-site. Peripheral rate 0.691 vs null 0.382 (perm p=0.0). Both heading (0.286 vs 0.191, p=0.0) and colophon (0.405 vs 0.191, p=0.0) positions are enriched, with colophon slightly stronger. All 6 sites with >=10 testable markers show significant same-direction (enriched) enrichment; survives leave-one-site-out on Haghia Triada (p=0.0). Upgrades structural read from 'standalone marker' to 'document-peripheral element (heading and/or colophon / section boundary)'.
key_findings: U+1076B is strongly document-peripheral: 69.1% of target markers sit on the FIRST or LAST content-line vs 38.2% expected under the within-inscription line-shuffle null (perm p=0.0, 2000 reshuffles).
Both heading (li==0: 28.6% vs 19.1% null, p=0.0) and colophon (li==L-1: 40.5% vs 19.1% null, p=0.0) positions are independently enriched; the colophon/last-line signal is the stronger of the two.

Cross-site robust: all 6 sites with >=10 testable markers (Haghia Triada, Khania, Zakros, Phaistos, Knossos, Arkhalkhori) are significantly enriched in the same direction; survives leave-one-site-out on the largest site (Haghia Triada, p=0.0).
Interior placement is DEPLETED (30.9% observed vs ~61.8% expected), ruling out an interior-separator read at the document level.

Positive control (synthetic) PASSED: detect p=0.0 (planted peripheral corpus flagged), false-positive rate 0.05<=0.10 (uniform placement not flagged across 20 draws); null mean validated against marker-weighted 2/L.
successor_hypotheses: E063: Does the colophon (last-line) enrichment concentrate on inscriptions ending in a numeric total (administrative summation colophon) vs non-numeric endings? (L2/L3 boundary).
E064: Is the heading (first-line) marker co-occurring with a specific following token class (e.g., a toponym or commodity word) that would mark it as a section header? (L2 bigram).
E065: On single-line (L<3) inscriptions, does the marker still appear, and does its line-isolation (E061) plus periphery here imply it is a one-line document boundary in those cases?
E066: Does the marker's peripheral position interact with support type (tablet vs seal vs other) — i.e., is the heading/colophon role support-dependent?
E067: Is the marker a section boundary WITHIN long inscriptions (recurring at internal major divisions marked by 'div') rather than only at document ends? (L2 internal segmentation).
E068: Compare the document-position profile of U+1076B against other frequent 'other' tokens to test whether periphery is a unique signature of this marker or generic to the 'other' class.
non_circular: Target is the anonymous token TYPE defined only by t=='other' AND '\U0001076b' in raw. Metric is pure document content-line index (li) within the inscription; no sign value, reading, phonetics, or meaning used. Null re-places line indices uniformly within each inscription, preserving marker count and L, so any enrichment is attributable to document position beyond within-inscription line geometry. Positive control is synthetic and stated as such. Verdict is mechanical from the frozen rule.
